req.title: Manager views document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated manager shall be able to view a document through the UI form.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def manager_user():
    """Return a user object with the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-001"
    user.username = "jane.manager"
    user.role = "manager"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_manager_user():
    """Return an authenticated user WITHOUT the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-002"
    user.username = "john.viewer"
    user.role = "viewer"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_document():
    """Return a document with all mandatory fields populated."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-123"
    doc.title = "Project Charter"
    doc.content = "This is the full content of the project charter."
    doc.created_by = "usr-001"
    doc.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0)
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def document_missing_title():
    """Return a document where the mandatory title field is missing."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-456"
    doc.title = None          # mandatory field missing
    doc.content = "Some content."
    doc.created_by = "usr-001"
    doc.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0)
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def document_missing_content():
    """Return a document where the mandatory content field is missing."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-789"
    doc.title = "Incomplete Document"
    doc.content = None        # mandatory field missing
    doc.created_by = "usr-001"
    doc.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0)
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def document_missing_multiple_fields():
    """Return a document missing several mandatory fields."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-999"
    doc.title = None
    doc.content = None
    doc.created_by = None
    doc.created_at = None
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def mock_document_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def mock_form():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def document_view(mock_document_service, mock_audit_service, mock_form):
    """
    Construct the system under test.

    Replace with the real import once the implementation exists, e.g.
        from myapp.views import DocumentView
    """
    from document_view import DocumentView          # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy import)
    return DocumentView(
        document_service=mock_document_service,
        audit_service=mock_audit_service,
        form=mock_form,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – build a reusable expected audit payload matcher
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def expected_audit_payload(user, document, action="VIEW_DOCUMENT"):
    return {
        "action": action,
        "user_id": user.id,
        "username": user.username,
        "document_id": document.id,
        "document_title": document.title,
        "status": "SUCCESS",
    }


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & Authorisation
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthenticationAndAuthorisation:

    def test_authenticated_manager_can_access_view(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document, mock_document_service
    ):
        """An authenticated manager must be granted access to the view endpoint."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_redirected(
        self, document_view, unauthenticated_user, valid_document
    ):
        """Unauthenticated users must NOT see the document; expect 401/redirect."""
        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=unauthenticated_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert response.status_code in (401, 302)

    def test_non_manager_role_is_forbidden(
        self, document_view, non_manager_user, valid_document
    ):
        """A user without the Manager role must receive a 403 Forbidden response."""
        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=non_manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, document_view, unauthenticated_user, valid_document, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record should be created for an unauthenticated access attempt."""
        document_view.view_document(
            user=unauthenticated_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_manager_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, document_view, non_manager_user, valid_document, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record should be created when authorisation fails."""
        document_view.view_document(
            user=non_manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Document Retrieval
# ===========================================================================

class TestDocumentRetrieval:

    def test_correct_document_is_fetched(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document, mock_document_service
    ):
        """The view must request the document identified by the supplied ID."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        mock_document_service.get_document.assert_called_once_with(valid_document.id)

    def test_nonexistent_document_returns_404(
        self, document_view, manager_user, mock_document_service
    ):
        """Requesting a document that does not exist must return 404."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = None

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id="doc-does-not-exist"
        )

        assert response.status_code == 404

    def test_nonexistent_document_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, document_view, manager_user, mock_document_service, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """A 404 (document not found) must NOT produce an audit record."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = None

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id="doc-does-not-exist"
        )

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Mandatory Field Validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    def test_valid_document_passes_validation(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """A fully populated document must pass all mandatory field checks."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_missing_title_fails_validation(
        self, document_view, manager_user, document_missing_title,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """A document without a title must fail mandatory field validation."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = document_missing_title
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (False, ["title is required"])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=document_missing_title.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert "title" in response.errors

    def test_missing_content_fails_validation(
        self, document_view, manager_user, document_missing_content,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """A document without content must fail mandatory field validation."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = document_missing_content
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (False, ["content is required"])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=document_missing_content.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        assert "content" in response.errors

    def test_multiple_missing_fields_reported_together(
        self, document_view, manager_user, document_missing_multiple_fields,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """All missing mandatory fields must be reported in a single response."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = document_missing_multiple_fields
        missing = ["title is required", "content is required", "created_by is required",
                   "created_at is required"]
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (False, missing)

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=document_missing_multiple_fields.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422
        for field_error in ("title", "content", "created_by", "created_at"):
            assert field_error in response.errors

    def test_validation_failure_does_not_write_audit_record(
        self, document_view, manager_user, document_missing_title,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """A validation failure must prevent an audit record from being written."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = document_missing_title
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (False, ["title is required"])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=document_missing_title.id
        )

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("field,value", [
        ("title", ""),
        ("title", "   "),        # whitespace-only
        ("content", ""),
        ("created_by", ""),
        ("version", ""),
    ])
    def test_empty_or_whitespace_mandatory_fields_fail_validation(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, field, value
    ):
        """Empty or whitespace-only values for mandatory fields must fail validation."""
        setattr(valid_document, field, value)
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (False, [f"{field} is required"])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 422


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Audit Record on Successful View
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecordOnSuccess:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_success(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """A single audit record must be written when a manager views a valid document."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        mock_audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_user_id(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must capture the viewing manager's user ID."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["user_id"] == manager_user.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_username(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must capture the viewing manager's username."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["username"] == manager_user.username

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_document_id(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must reference the viewed document's ID."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["document_id"] == valid_document.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_document_title(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must capture the document title for traceability."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["document_title"] == valid_document.title

    def test_audit_record_action_is_view_document(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit action code must be VIEW_DOCUMENT."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["action"] == "VIEW_DOCUMENT"

    def test_audit_record_status_is_success(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record status field must be SUCCESS on a successful view."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_payload["status"] == "SUCCESS"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must include a timestamp of when the view occurred."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert "timestamp" in audit_payload
        assert audit_payload["timestamp"] is not None

    def test_audit_record_timestamp_is_recent(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """The audit timestamp must be close to the time the view was performed."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        before = datetime.utcnow()
        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )
        after = datetime.utcnow()

        audit_payload = mock_audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert before <= audit_payload["timestamp"] <= after

    def test_audit_service_failure_does_not_suppress_success_response(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """
        If the audit service raises an exception the view should still return 200
        (the audit layer must not break the user experience), but the error must
        be surfaced in a way the platform can detect (e.g. logged or re-raised
        internally). Adjust this assertion to match your error strategy.
        """
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])
        mock_audit_service.write.side_effect = RuntimeError("Audit DB unavailable")

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        # The document must still be displayed to the manager.
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_audit_write_called_exactly_once_per_view(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form, mock_audit_service
    ):
        """Exactly one audit record must be written per successful document view."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert mock_audit_service.write.call_count == 1


# ===========================================================================
# 5. UI Form – Content Rendering
# ===========================================================================

class TestUIFormRendering:

    def test_response_contains_document_title(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """The rendered form must display the document's title."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert valid_document.title in response.body

    def test_response_contains_document_content(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """The rendered form must display the document's content."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert valid_document.content in response.body

    def test_response_contains_document_version(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """The rendered form must display the document's version."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        response = document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        assert valid_document.version in response.body

    def test_form_is_rendered_with_document_data(
        self, document_view, manager_user, valid_document,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """The UI form must be populated (bound) with the fetched document data."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = valid_document
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (True, [])

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=valid_document.id
        )

        mock_form.populate.assert_called_once_with(valid_document)

    def test_validation_errors_displayed_in_form(
        self, document_view, manager_user, document_missing_title,
        mock_document_service, mock_form
    ):
        """Validation error messages must be passed back to the form for display."""
        mock_document_service.get_document.return_value = document_missing_title
        errors = ["title is required"]
        mock_form.validate.return_value = (False, errors)

        document_view.view_document(
            user=manager_user, document_id=document_missing_title.id
        )

        mock_form.set_errors.assert_called_once_with(errors)
```

---

### Notes for implementers

| Area | Expectation |
|---|---|
| **`DocumentView`** | Accepts `document_service`, `audit_service`, and `form` via constructor injection. |
| **`view_document` response** | Must expose `.status_code`, `.body` (str), and `.errors` (dict/list) attributes. |
| **`mock_form.populate`** | Should bind document data to all UI fields before rendering. |
| **`mock_form.set_errors`** | Should attach validation messages to the form so they are visible in the UI. |
| **Audit payload** | Must contain at minimum: `action`, `user_id`, `username`, `document_id`, `document_title`, `status`, `timestamp`. |
| **`AuditService.write`** | First positional argument must be the audit payload `dict`. |

"""
Pytest tests for requirement:
An authenticated manager shall be able to view a document through the UI form.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def manager_user():
    """Return a user object with the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-001"
    user.username = "jane.manager"
    user.role = "manager"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_manager_user():
    """Return an authenticated user WITHOUT the Manager role."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = "usr-002"
    user.username = "john.viewer"
    user.role = "viewer"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def valid_document():
    """Return a document with all mandatory fields populated."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-123"
    doc.title = "Project Charter"
    doc.content = "This is the full content of the project charter."
    doc.created_by = "usr-001"
    doc.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0)
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def document_missing_title():
    """Return a document where the mandatory title field is missing."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-456"
    doc.title = None          # mandatory field missing
    doc.content = "Some content."
    doc.created_by = "usr-001"
    doc.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0)
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def document_missing_content():
    """Return a document where the mandatory content field is missing."""
    doc = MagicMock()
    doc.id = "doc-789"
    doc.title = "Incomplete Document"
    doc.content = None        # mandatory field missing
    doc.created_by = "usr-001"
    doc.created_at = datetime(2024, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0)
    doc.status = "active"
    doc.version = "1.0"
    return doc


@pytest.fixture
def document_missing_multiple_fields():
    """Return a document missing several mandatory fields."""
    doc = MagicMock()

def expected_audit_payload(user, document, action="VIEW_DOCUMENT"):
    return {
        "action": action,
        "user_id": user.id,
        "username": user.username,
        "document_id": document.id,
        "document_title": document.title,
        "status": "SUCCESS",
    }
